text:
*[Bug 19086] nlminb() no longer works reliably with bounds*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19086>

Peter Dalgaard (<mailto:pd.mes@cbs.dk|pd.mes@cbs.dk>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |pd.mes@cbs.dk

--- Comment #5 from Peter Dalgaard (<mailto:pd.mes@cbs.dk|pd.mes@cbs.dk>) ---
Thanks Martin!

Could Kasper perhaps provide a pointer to exactly what was the issue with
Fortran standards? I have a nagging feeling that we might have more of that
sort lurking around. 

I seem to recall that it used to be fairly common to have Fortran code like
f(X, Y) with a note that Y can be the same as X because the algorithm allowed
in-place operation. But maybe I am just getting old....

-pd

elements.text: [Bug 19086] nlminb() no longer works reliably with bounds

) ---
Thanks Martin!

Could Kasper perhaps provide a pointer to exactly what was the issue with
Fortran standards? I have a nagging feeling that we might have more of that
sort lurking around. 

I seem to recall that it used to be fairly common to have Fortran code like
f(X, Y) with a note that Y can be the same as X because the algorithm allowed
in-place operation. But maybe I am just getting old....

-pd

text:
*[Bug 19086] nlminb() no longer works reliably with bounds*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19086>

--- Comment #6 from Kasper Kristensen (<mailto:kaskr@dtu.dk|kaskr@dtu.dk>) ---
Maybe not the best reference, but this is a good description of the problem:

<https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E19205-01/819-5262/auto35/index.html>

And yes, it does seem to be common. Looking just at 'portsrc.f', it has this
(or similar) comment in many of its subroutines:

"X AND Y MAY OCCUPY THE SAME STORAGE."

elements.text: [Bug 19086] nlminb() no longer works reliably with bounds

) ---

text:
*[Bug 18010] all.equal.function fails when fun envs contain `...`*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18010>

Gabriel Becker (<mailto:gabembecker@gmail.com|gabembecker@gmail.com>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |gabembecker@gmail.com

--- Comment #25 from Gabriel Becker (<mailto:gabembecker@gmail.com|gabembecker@gmail.com>) ---
This issue was resolved some time before 4.5.1 (the version I have immediately
handy):

```
> a <- (function(...) function() NULL)(1)
>     b <- (function(...) function() NULL)(1)
>     all.equal(a, b)
[1] TRUE
>
> R.version
               _                           
platform       aarch64-apple-darwin20      
arch           aarch64                     
os             darwin20                    
system         aarch64, darwin20           
status                                     
major          4                           
minor          5.1                         
year           2025                        
month          06                          
day            13                          
svn rev        88306                       
language       R                           
version.string R version 4.5.1 (2025-06-13)

```

The issue can be closed as FIXED

probably be moved if it is still fresh.
